Package com.flarerobotics.lib.control

Class ShooterCalculator.ShooterConfig

java.lang.Object
com.flarerobotics.lib.control.ShooterCalculator.ShooterConfig
Enclosing class:
ShooterCalculator
public static class ShooterCalculator.ShooterConfig extends Object
Configuration inputs for the shooter mechanism.

  • Constructor Details

    • ShooterConfig

      public ShooterConfig(double barrelLength, double pivotHeight, double wheelRadius, edu.wpi.first.math.interpolation.InterpolatingDoubleTreeMap dropFractionMap, double maxFreeRPM, double maxAngle, double minAngle, double efficiency, double nominalVoltage, Supplier<Double> pivotHorizontalRotationSupplier, double[] distances, double[] heightDiffs, double[][] angles, double[][] freeSpeeds)
      Constructs a new fully-empirical (see ShooterCalculator.CalculationStrategy) ShooterConfig.

      Example Arrays: 

       
 // Sorted Arrays
 double[] horizontalDistances = { 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, 5.0 }; // meters
 double[] heightDiffs = { -0.5, 0.0, +0.5, +1.0 }; // meters, relative to the end point of the hood/barrel
 double[][] angleGrid = {
         // -----------------> HeightDiff (-0.5 -> +1.0)
         { 45, 40, 35, 30 }, // at d=2.0
         { 42, 38, 33, 28 }, // at d=3.0
         { 39, 35, 31, 27 }, // at d=4.0
         { 37, 33, 29, 25 }, // at d=5.0
 };
 double[][] rpmGrid = {
         // -------------------------> HeightDiff (-0.5 -> +1.0)
         { 3000, 3100, 3200, 3300 }, // at d=2.0
         { 2900, 3000, 3100, 3200 }, // at d=3.0
         { 2800, 2900, 3000, 3100 }, // at d=4.0
         { 2700, 2800, 2900, 3000 }, // at d=5.0
 };

      Notes:

      - The emprical data must be measured from the shooter's pivot point.

      - The grids must be sorted in ascending order.

      - The data should be large enough to avoid extrapolation due to out-of-bounds data, which may lead to significant inacuracies.

      - The RPM speeds use the flywheel RPM.

      Parameters:
      barrelLength - The length of the barrel/hood in meters.
      pivotHeight - The baseline height (pivot height) in meters.
      wheelRadius - The radius of the flywheel in meters.
      dropFractionMap - The fractional drop from unloaded to loaded RPM: (RPM_unloaded - RPM_loaded) / RPM_unloaded. Not used when the calculation mode is fully empirical. Map of unloaded RPM to the drop fraction.
      maxFreeRPM - The maximum free RPM of the flywheel.
      maxAngle - The maximum angle of the hood in degrees.
      minAngle - The minimum angle of the hood in degrees.
      efficiency - The efficiency of the system on a scale of 0->1. Defined as: v_real / v_theory. (RPM -> Velocity conversion efficiency)
      nominalVoltage - The nominal voltage of the battery.
      pivotHorizontalRotationSupplier - The supplier for the horizontal rotation of the shooter pivot in degrees, in account to robot rotation. Use the robot's heading if the shooter doesn't pivot horizontally. Otherwise, add the horizontal angle of the shooter to the robot's heading.
      distances - The horizontal distances in meters for the empirical map, with width M.
      heightDiffs - The height differences in meters for the empirical map, with width N.
      angles - The angles in degrees for the empirical map, in WxH = NxM size. Must be sorted.
      freeSpeeds - The free speeds in RPM for the empirical map, in WxH = NxM size. Must be sorted.

    • ShooterConfig

      public ShooterConfig(double barrelLength, double pivotHeight, double wheelRadius, edu.wpi.first.math.interpolation.InterpolatingDoubleTreeMap dropFractionMap, double maxFreeRPM, double maxAngle, double minAngle, double efficiency, double nominalVoltage, Supplier<Double> pivotHorizontalRotationSupplier, double[] distances, double[] heightDiffs, double[][] freeSpeeds)
      Constructs a new partially-empirical (see ShooterCalculator.CalculationStrategy) ShooterConfig. Preferrably use ShooterCalculator.CalculationStrategy.FULLY_EMPIRICAL instead.

      Example Arrays: 

       
 // Sorted Arrays
 double[] horizontalDistances = { 2.0, 3.0, 4.0, 5.0 }; // meters
 double[] heightDiffs = { -0.5, 0.0, +0.5, +1.0 }; // meters, relative to the end point of the hood/barrel
 double[][] rpmGrid = {
         // -------------------------> HeightDiff (-0.5 -> +1.0)
         { 3000, 3100, 3200, 3300 }, // at d=2.0
         { 2900, 3000, 3100, 3200 }, // at d=3.0
         { 2800, 2900, 3000, 3100 }, // at d=4.0
         { 2700, 2800, 2900, 3000 }, // at d=5.0
 };

      Notes:

      - The emprical data must be measured from the shooter's pivot point.

      - The angle grid must be sorted in ascending order.

      - The data should be large enough to avoid extrapolation due to out-of-bounds data.

      - The RPM speeds use the flywheel RPM.

      Parameters:
      barrelLength - The length of the barrel/hood in meters.
      pivotHeight - The baseline height (pivot height) in meters.
      wheelRadius - The radius of the flywheel in meters.
      dropFractionMap - The fractional drop from unloaded to loaded RPM: (RPM_unloaded - RPM_loaded) / RPM_unloaded. Not used when the calculation mode is fully empirical. Map of unloaded RPM to the drop fraction.
      maxFreeRPM - The maximum free RPM of the flywheel.
      maxAngle - The maximum angle of the hood in degrees.
      minAngle - The minimum angle of the hood in degrees.
      efficiency - The efficiency of the system on a scale of 0->1. Defined as: v_real / v_theory. (RPM -> Velocity conversion efficiency)
      nominalVoltage - The nominal voltage of the battery.
      pivotHorizontalRotationSupplier - The supplier for the horizontal rotation of the shooter pivot in degrees, in account to robot rotation. Use the robot's heading if the shooter doesn't pivot horizontally. Otherwise, add the horizontal angle of the shooter to the robot's heading.
      distances - The horizontal distances in meters for the empirical map, with width M.
      heightDiffs - The height differences in meters for the empirical map, with width N.
      freeSpeeds - The free speeds in RPM for the empirical map, in WxH = NxM size. Must be sorted.
